info.name: Part-48.3 Core Master Soft Delete Restore Tests
info.description: Master Postman test cases for CSRM Payroll System Part-48.3. Covers active list, deleted list, soft delete, restore, route order, audit log, and validation checks for core master modules.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 200", function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test("Response success is true", function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.success).to.eql(true); });
pm.test("Status code is 200", function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test("Response success is true", function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.success).to.eql(true); });
pm.test("Soft delete status code is 200", function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

pm.test("Record is soft deleted", function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.success).to.eql(true); pm.expect(json.data.isDeleted).to.eql(true); pm.expect(json.data.deletedAt).to.exist; });

item.name: Company - Get Active By ID After Delete Should Fail

exec: pm.test("Deleted record should not be returned as active record", function () { pm.expect([400, 404]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
pm.test("Status code is 200", function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test("Response success is true", function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.success).to.eql(true); });
pm.test("Restore status code is 200", function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

pm.test("Record is restored", function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.success).to.eql(true); pm.expect(json.data.isDeleted).to.eql(false); pm.expect(json.data.restoredAt).to.exist; });

item.name: Manual Conflict Checks
item.description: Manual flow: delete a test record, create a second active record with the same unique key, then try restore. Expected HTTP 409 conflict.

exec: pm.test("Expected conflict or success depending on setup", function () { pm.expect([200,409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
pm.test("Expected conflict or success depending on setup", function () { pm.expect([200,409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
